purpose: Legacy Portal 1-style runtime controller adapted for Portal 2 data. It waits for DOMContentLoaded, attaches splash dismissal handlers, then on user click/Space draws text borders, starts Want You Gone audio, types lyrics and credits, and blinks cursors.
invariants: cake.init must run after the HTML containers exist; it draws borders before scheduling line rendering.
processLyricLine and processCreditLine assume global lyrics and credits arrays are loaded before this file.
creditsMaxTime and creditsStartTime determine the full credit scroll pacing from total character count.
Border character counts in drawLyricsBorder and drawCreditsBorder must stay aligned with the CSS box widths.
cake.init runs only after the user dismisses the #splash overlay (click or Space) on DOMContentLoaded; this satisfies modern autoplay policy.
gotchas: The file body is wrapped in an IIFE with 'use strict'; cake is exposed to the page via window.cake = cake.
initMusicPlayer declares delay twice, both effectively zero, and uses a local Want You Gone.mp3 path.
setTimeout uses the 3-argument form (callback, delay, ...args) to capture loop-iteration values at schedule time so var-scoped loop variables do not produce the closure trap.
The DOMContentLoaded handler assumes index.html provides an element with id 'splash'; without it splash.addEventListener throws and the credits never start.

key_functions.name: init
key_functions.summary: Caches DOM containers, draws text borders, initializes credits and blinkers, starts audio, then schedules rendering.

key_functions.name: processLyricLine
key_functions.summary: Creates lyric spans in chronological order, handles clear markers, and schedules per-character typing.

key_functions.name: processCreditLine
key_functions.summary: Maintains the visible credit window and schedules per-character credit typing.
